reason: The statement applies because the events described in the text involve a series of interactions between King Hezekiah of Judah and the king of Assyria, culminating in a significant change and the breaking of the series when the angel of the LORD smote the Assyrian camp, leading to the retreat of Sennacherib and his eventual demise at the hands of his sons. This event marked a turning point and the end of the Assyrian threat against Jerusalem.
